channel.title: News on CEDIS
channel.description: Recent content in News on CEDIS

item.title: CEDIS launches Deck Manager, an interactive presentation platform with privacy by design
item.description: New CEDIS platform for interactive presentations, available at slides.cedis.tec.br and deck.cedis.tec.br.

item.title: CEDIS students' thesis gives rise to Git Ranking, a gamified application for agile teams on GitHub
item.description: A bachelor's thesis supervised by Prof. Sergio Freitas resulted in Git Ranking, a gamified application integrated with GitHub.

item.title: Paper on EduTrack's diagnostic instrument accepted at SBIE 2026
item.description: Paper (submission #27258) authored by Sergio Freitas, Daniel Rodrigues da Rocha, Davi Rodrigues da Rocha, and Mylena Angélica Silva Farias was accepted at SBIE 2026.

item.title: Paper on DevDog accepted at the SBES 2026 Tools Track
item.description: A paper stemming from Luciano Ricardo da Silva Junior's bachelor's thesis was accepted at SBES 2026, supervised by Prof. Sergio Freitas and co-supervised by Prof. André Lanna.

item.title: CEDIS students' thesis gives rise to Atloria, an augmented reality app for teaching data structures
item.description: A bachelor's thesis supervised by Prof. Sergio Freitas resulted in Atloria, an educational augmented reality app.

item.title: DevDog: undergraduate thesis turns code smells into gamified learning
item.description: Luciano Ricardo da Silva Junior defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis that gave rise to DevDog, a gamified web application for teaching code smell identification.

item.title: OctaAnalysis platform expands CEDIS's portfolio of digital innovation
item.description: OctaAnalysis is a CEDIS platform focused on the design, management, and analytical monitoring of gamified experiences.

item.title: CEDIS presents Evidentia Review for systematic literature reviews
item.description: Evidentia Review is a CEDIS software platform designed for end-to-end systematic literature reviews.

item.title: Contextus software registration is granted by INPI
item.description: The Contextus certificate was issued on February 18, 2026, formalizing the software's legal protection before INPI.

item.title: DevOps under the DORA lens: a portrait from the financial sector
item.description: A dissertation defended at UnB’s Applied Computing graduate program proposes a framework to assess DevOps maturity in banking teams by combining DORA metrics, document analysis and sociotechnical triangulation.

item.title: UnB’s Software Engineering Program hosts Gamification Team from Banco do Brasil
item.description: UnB’s Software Engineering Program hosts Gamification Team from Banco do Brasil

item.title: CEDIS at UnB: recognition and innovation
item.description: CEDIS celebrates the achievement of Professor Sergio Freitas, who received an Honorable Mention in the Annual Undergraduate Teaching Innovation Award (DEG/UnB), in the category Use of technological resources and production of innovative teaching material.

item.title: Volunteer call for CEDIS – Applications open
item.description: CEDIS opens applications for volunteer students interested in participating in research projects and academic activities.

item.title: Prof. Sergio Freitas leads pedagogical training at UFES with strong participation
item.description: Professor Sergio Freitas conducted a pedagogical training course at UFES for faculty members in the fields of Exact Sciences and Engineering, with broad participation and practical application of the content covered.

item.title: Gamifying Scrum: an undergraduate thesis maps the literature and drafts guidelines for the field
item.description: Denniel Roriz Lima defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis running a bibliometric and thematic review on the use of gamification in agile processes with Scrum, and proposing practical guidelines for implementation.

item.title: AgroMart migrates from IaaS to serverless: student duo shows small farmers can stay inside the free tier
item.description: Kalebe Cunha and Murilo Santana defended in February 2025 at UnB’s Engineering Faculty an undergraduate thesis that migrates AgroMart from IaaS to AWS Lambda and tests the feasibility of running the app within the free tier — dropping cost for farmers.

item.title: Article awarded Best Paper at ITHET 2024
item.description: Paper presented at IEEE ITHET 2024 in Paris receives Best Paper award.

item.title: Article on gamified chatbot development process presented at international conference
item.description: Article presented at IntelliSys 2024, in Amsterdam, Netherlands.

item.title: CEDIS represented at RNP Forum 2024
item.description: Prof. Daniel Sundfeld presented the Laguna project at the RNP Forum 2024.

item.title: Canal Ciência project wins best paper award at international conference
item.description: Paper was presented at the 24th ICCSA in Hanoi, Vietnam.

item.title: Software engineering student and CEDIS fellow presents paper at international conference
item.description: Paper presented at the 24th ICCSA in Hanoi, Vietnam.

item.title: UFPA Student Undertakes Internship at CEDIS
item.description: UFPA student undertakes an internship at CEDIS focusing on gamification for literacy

item.title: CEDIS Launches Contextus Software
item.description: Contextus - An advanced AI, NLP, and ML platform at the heart of Scientific Analysis, led by CEDIS.

item.title: CEDIS Professors Receive Honorable Mention
item.description: Contextus - An advanced AI, NLP, and ML platform at the heart of Scientific Analysis, led by CEDIS

item.title: CEDIS Signs International Agreement
item.description: The agreement between CEDIS and LabVista (Portugal) aims to promote significant advances in technological and academic research.

item.title: CEDIS Researchers Launch Book
item.description: Book on Learning Indicators launched with the participation of CEDIS researchers.

item.title: CEDIS Participates in Gamification Project
item.description: Partnership between CEDIS and IBICT launches an innovative gamification project for the Canal Ciência portal

item.title: CEDIS Gamification Innovates HCI Teaching at FCTE
item.description: IHC Journey - An RPG adventure in learning the Human-Computer Interaction course.

item.title: CEDIS Gamification Revolutionizes Software Teaching at FCTE
item.description: Innovative gamification by Prof. Sergio Freitas engages Software Engineering students with a playful and effective approach.

item.title: Early stage startups and software maintenance an undergraduate thesis documents the state of practice
item.description: Filipe Toyoshima Silva defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis mapping how early-stage startups handle software maintenance — a phase dominated by scarce resources and high change volume.

item.title: What users say when no one asks: an undergraduate thesis mines Play Store reviews for usability signals
item.description: Augusto Moreno Vilarins Cardoso da Silva and Ícaro Pereira de Oliveira defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis designing a semi-automated process to extract usability signals from user comments on the Google Play Store.

item.title: Analytics inside the classroom: an undergraduate thesis uses data mining to decide where to tune TBL
item.description: Allan Jefrey Pereira Nobre defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis applying Learning Analytics and Educational Data Mining to support pedagogical decisions in Team-Based Learning courses.

item.title: A junior enterprise, a live experiment: an undergraduate thesis evaluates gamification applied on the ground
item.description: Ateldy Borges Brasil Filho defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis evaluating gamification applied in a junior enterprise — an organization with rotating teams, short cycles, and high initial motivation.

item.title: Interfaces that induce error: an undergraduate thesis catalogues common traps — and how to avoid them
item.description: Caio César de Almeida Beleza defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis analyzing software interface problems that push users toward error and systematizing recommendations to avoid them.

item.title: Reading what's already logged: an undergraduate thesis proposes planning process improvements from issues
item.description: Sannya Santana de Arvelos defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis proposing the use of issue analysis as the basis for identifying software process improvement opportunities.

item.title: Software process improvement: where to start when everything seems chaotic?
item.description: Rafael Contessotto Bragança Pinheiro defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis proposing an approach to help organizations kick off software process improvement programs — the hardest step when almost everything demands attention.

item.title: Exploratory testing in action: an undergraduate thesis pairs scripted and free approaches to evaluate software
item.description: Iolane Caroline Alves de Andrade defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis applying exploratory testing alongside traditional testing, highlighting the learning gain that exploratory testing brings to the process.

item.title: Refactoring as leverage: an undergraduate thesis shows how to rewrite code for variability
item.description: Ronyell Henrique dos Santos defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis applying refactoring operations to improve software system variability — reducing maintenance costs over the lifecycle.

item.title: Team based learning gets a platform undergraduate thesis moves the methodology from the classroom to the cloud
item.description: Victor Hugo Arnaud Deon defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis developing PGTBL — an open-source platform to manage Team-Based Learning activities in university courses.

item.title: Who wrote that commit? An undergraduate thesis computes authorship rates inside Git projects
item.description: Laércio Silva de Sousa Júnior defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis investigating ways to compute developer authorship rates inside Git-versioned projects.

item.title: Measure to engage: an undergraduate thesis proposes a strategy to improve gamified applications
item.description: Paulo Markes Rodrigues de Oliveira Calado defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis developing a strategy to identify improvement opportunities in gamified applications — tackling the problem that the initial shine does not always translate into prolonged use.

item.title: Drivers as sensors: an undergraduate thesis proposes a crowdsourced app to monitor city fleets
item.description: Jonathan Paiva and Luis Henrique Guimarães defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis replacing sensors and cameras with human collaboration to monitor cars across a city — the NotifiCar app.

item.title: Making the invisible visible: an undergraduate thesis proposes a method to expose technical debt in agile teams
item.description: Iago Rodrigues Gonçalves defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis investigating how to identify, measure and communicate technical debt — a problem that gains weight in agile methodologies focused on continuous delivery.

item.title: Attracting is easy, retaining is gamification: an undergraduate thesis fights churn in an academic social network
item.description: Tiago Ribeiro de Assunção defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis applying gamification to the About Social Network to fight its weak spot: the network attracts new users easily but fails to retain them.

item.title: Math, children, and digital victories: the software that wants to turn a class into a mission
item.description: Leandro Alves Ferreira Santos defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis developing gamified software to support math teaching for children — a territory poorly served by edtech products.

item.title: Experience bases: what to do with the knowledge that walks in and out of R&D labs
item.description: Attany Nathaly Lima Araújo defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis designing a process for building experience bases in R&D labs — a classic knowledge management challenge applied to software engineering.

item.title: Ciência sem Fronteiras through an engineering lens: what students brought back besides English
item.description: Leandro Veloso Rodrigues defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis investigating the impact of Brazil’s Ciência sem Fronteiras program on the soft skills of future software engineers.

item.title: A tool to help teams build gamification projects without starting from scratch
item.description: Ludimila da Bela Cruz defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis proposing a tool to support teams building gamification projects, anchoring the process in structured motivational elements.

item.title: Before the generative AI boom, an undergraduate thesis was already teaching machines to grade essays
item.description: Back in 2016, well before the LLM frenzy, Cristóvão Frinhani defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis that uses semantic similarity and machine learning to support distance-learning tutors grading essay questions.

item.title: Algorithms with points: an undergraduate thesis adapts a virtual environment to gamified programming teaching
item.description: Wilker Mesquita de Assunção defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis adapting a virtual learning environment to teach algorithms using gamification techniques.

item.title: Choosing a CMS without falling back on 'that's what we already use'
item.description: Thiago Silveira Honorato defended an undergraduate thesis proposing a rational method to choose between Content Management Systems for different scenarios — reducing reliance on decisions based on team familiarity alone.

item.title: Milliseconds matter: an undergraduate thesis measures the impact of code choices on Android
item.description: Victor Hugo Alves de Carvalho defended in 2015 at UnB Gama an empirical undergraduate thesis comparing programming techniques on Android and measuring the effect of each on app performance and responsiveness.

item.title: Ontologies to help mapmakers: an undergraduate thesis tackles the hardest step of systematic mapping
item.description: Igor de Araújo Ramos defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis proposing the use of ontologies to support the thorniest step of systematic mappings: turning collected data into a knowledge map that is actually usable.
